summary.intent: Implement all missing MS Teams channel features compared to OpenClaw (JWT auth, Adaptive Cards, retry logic, streaming, Graph API, etc.), improve onboarding and documentation for Teams setup, and keep the branch in sync with main.
summary.outcome: A comprehensive MS Teams channel implementation was delivered across 8 new modules and 10 modified files, including JWT verification, edit-in-place streaming, message chunking, Adaptive Cards, Graph API agent tools, retry with backoff, attachment handling, per-team config overrides, full documentation, and Tailscale/ngrok-aware onboarding. The branch was merged with main twice cleanly.

repo: Channel capabilities are declared in `crates/channels/src/plugin.rs` as a descriptor struct with boolean flags (supports_interactive, supports_threads, supports_reactions); updating this is required when adding new channel features.

Config validation in `validate.rs` uses `Map(Box::new(Leaf))` for channel configs — semantic validation is deferred to serde deserialization of the typed config struct, so no changes to validate.rs are needed when adding new fields.
The gateway tool registry in `crates/gateway/src/server.rs` registers tools after `SendMessageTool`; new agent tools need both a module in `gateway/src/` and a registration call there.
The `RedactedConfig` serialization uses a hardcoded field count that must be kept in sync manually — it was wrong (9 instead of 14) and would have caused runtime serialization panics.

The onboarding data flow: `preferredPublicBaseUrl()` helper (added in main's ngrok PR) is the canonical way for channel forms to get the configured public URL; channel-specific tunnel logic should not be re-implemented per channel.
Default `offered` channels list is in `crates/config/src/schema.rs` and requires updating tests when adding new channels.
JS locale files are only populated for Telegram; all other channels including Teams have missing translations — this is a known cross-cutting gap.

code.finding: RedactedConfig had hardcoded count of 9 instead of 14 always-present fields — runtime serialization would panic. Count must be manually kept in sync with actual struct fields.
std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard held across .await points prevents the future from being Send. Guards must be explicitly scoped and dropped before any await point.
Teams webhook handler must return 202 Accepted immediately and process in tokio::spawn to avoid Teams retrying on slow LLM responses. The teams_plugin Arc must be cloned before the spawn closure.

After merging main (which added RemoteAccessStep with ngrok/Tailscale), TeamsForm had duplicate tunnel logic — old Tailscale-specific fetch/enable code plus the new preferredPublicBaseUrl() useEffect. Post-merge cleanup was required to remove the redundant old logic.

workflow: For large multi-file implementations, reading all files first in parallel before writing any saves context and avoids contradictions between modules.
Clippy issues at scale (collapsible-if, unwrap_in_tests, field_reassign_with_default across 8 new files) are best handled with a dedicated subagent rather than fixing inline.
When merging main that touches the same files, the merge succeeds cleanly but leaves duplicate logic that must be manually reconciled — checking for functional overlap post-merge is important.
API surface methods not yet called from other crates generate dead_code warnings; adding #[allow(dead_code)] is appropriate for intentional future-facing public API.
Test count differences between runs (171 vs 172) can be transient; re-running to confirm is sufficient rather than investigating immediately.

friction: std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard held across .await in plugin.rs caused a Send bound failure that was only caught at httpd crate compilation, not msteams crate compilation.
SendOutcome enum visibility (pub vs pub(crate)) required an extra iteration — declared private, used from plugin, compilation failed.
The RedactedConfig field count bug was a silent correctness issue that would only surface at runtime — not caught by tests or compilation.
After merging main, TeamsForm ended up with both the old Tailscale-specific banner/fetch logic and the new preferredPublicBaseUrl() useEffect, requiring a post-merge cleanup pass.
Clippy generated many warnings across 8 new files (collapsible-if, unwrap_in_tests, field_reassign_with_default) requiring a subagent pass to fix systematically.
chunking.rs test `splits_at_space` was incorrect — the expected value didn't account for the rfind behavior at the boundary, caught only when running tests.
open_items: ngrok native Rust SDK integration as a public access option alongside Tailscale Funnel — deferred to a separate PR.
Localization (fr/zh) for all Teams UI strings — cross-cutting gap affecting all channels, not Teams-specific.
E2E Playwright tests for Teams channel setup flow — no channel currently has dedicated e2e tests.

Graph API agent tools (search, pins, member-info) are wired but the corresponding Graph API permissions (Chat.Read.All, User.Read.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All) need to be documented in the bot setup guide as optional prerequisites.
Per-team/channel config override UI — the backend supports nested `teams.<teamId>.channels.<channelId>` overrides but the web UI has no editor for them; users must edit TOML directly.
